system: point displacement sensor over a three-mode fixed-end string truncation

state_variables.name: q1
state_variables.meaning: amplitude of first fixed-end sine mode

state_variables.name: q2
state_variables.meaning: amplitude of second fixed-end sine mode

state_variables.name: q3
state_variables.meaning: amplitude of third fixed-end sine mode

ideal: point displacement y at x0 = L_string / 4 as a linear functional of q1, q2, q3
practical: single optical displacement pickup, contact displacement probe, or microphone-like point channel calibrated at x0 = L_string / 4

regime_assumptions: same small-amplitude fixed-end three-mode truncation as string_fixed_end_three_mode
point sensor is calibrated to a linear displacement measurement at x0 = L_string / 4
observer row is C_n = sqrt(2 / L_string) sin(n pi x0 / L_string) for n = 1, 2, 3
sensor reads one scalar displacement channel, not full modal access
modes above n = 3, damping, drive, noise, and point-spread effects are outside this card
no hidden-load call is licensed because no ceiling/reference is declared
equation_hook: y = sum_{n=1}^3 sqrt(2 / L_string) sin(n pi x0 / L_string) q_n; U(q) = 1/2 q^T K q

units_and_dimensions.q_n: m sqrt(m) under the declared orthonormal spatial basis convention
units_and_dimensions.Phi_y: quotient stiffness for the declared point displacement channel

coordinate_note: This card is the first practical sensor-over-modal observer. The point sensor row is not full modal access; it induces a one-dimensional quotient stiffness for the measured displacement channel. Frequencies still require the separate pair (K, M), and hidden load still requires a declared ceiling/reference.

module_object.description: three-mode stiffness Hessian K observed through one point-displacement row C

derivation_status: exact for the declared three-mode fixed-end truncation and point sensor position

observer_map.description: observe y at x0 = L_string / 4 through the first three sine modes

ceiling_or_reference.description: no visible-block ceiling or reference stiffness is declared; hidden-load is therefore refused by omission

positive_scope: This card shows that a practical point sensor becomes a declared observer map over a finite modal object and exposes a one-dimensional quotient stiffness.
boundary: It does not infer full modal amplitudes, attach modes above n = 3, model sensor noise, attach damping or drive response, or license hidden-load structure.
failure_mode: The attachment fails if the sensor is not well approximated by a linear point displacement at the declared location, if the finite modal truncation is invalid, or if the point sensor is treated as full state access.
next_measurement: Place a calibrated displacement pickup near x0 = L_string / 4 and compare the local point response against the finite-mode quotient stiffness before adding noise or hidden-load structure.
